name: strip-ansi
usage_summary: Used in four source files across two packages. In cli-kit: ConcurrentOutput.tsx calls stripAnsi() on each chunk of subprocess output before splitting into display lines; output.ts imports it (likely for length calculations or clean log output). In e2e: helpers/strip-ansi.ts re-exports it as a named export to work around the ESM-only default export; create-test-apps.ts imports that re-export to clean CLI output during test app creation.
how_to_remove: The strip-ansi v7+ package is ESM-only and exports a single function that applies the regex /[\u001B\u009B][[\]()#;?]*(?:(?:(?:(?:;[-a-zA-Z\d\/#&.:=?%@~_]+)*|[a-zA-Z\d]+(?:;[-a-zA-Z\d\/#&.:=?%@~_]*)*)?)|(?:(?:\d{1,4}(?:;\d{0,4})*)?[\dA-PR-TZcf-nq-uy=><~]))/g to the input string. This one-liner can be inlined as a shared utility (e.g. in cli-kit/src/private/node/ansi.ts) and imported wherever needed. The e2e re-export wrapper already isolates the dependency surface.
replacement_reason: The entire package is a single regex applied to a string. Inlining the regex as a small utility function eliminates the dependency with zero behaviour change and removes the ESM interop wrapper currently needed in the e2e package.
